title: AvailabilityEnvelope
description: Availability envelope containing availabilities, sales Sales Locations and errors.

classUnitKey.description: Class Unit Key gives context when invoking the AvailabilityClassifications API.

classUnitCode.description:
`classUnitCode` is the unique identifier of a Class Unit when
combined with a `classUnitType`.

A valid `classUnitCode` always has a pattern like `^([A-Z]|[0-9]){2,5}$`.

This can either be a:

* **Retail Unit** - Retail Unit must be combined with a valid `classUnitCode`. For example, `SE` for Sweden or `DE` for Germany. A list of valid Class Units can be found [in CBD](https://iwww.cbdview.ikea.com/) (requires VPN or internal network).
* **Store ID** - The 3-digit store ID. E.g. `066`.

classUnitType.description:
Class Unit Type:
  * `RU` - Retail Unit - Retail Unit must be combined with a valid `classUnitCode`. For example, `SE` for Sweden or `DE` for Germany. A list of valid Retail Units can be found [in CBD](https://iwww.cbdview.ikea.com/) (requires VPN or internal network).
  * `STO` - Store - The store availability for a given store

itemKey.description: Item key (Product ID and Item type)

itemKey.description: Item key (Product ID and Item type)

items.description: Error object describing the reason of failure

code.description:
Error Codes:
  * `404` - Not found
  * `405` - ClassUnitCode doesn't exist
  * `602` - itemNos in query is required
  * `604` - itemNos.`n` in query should be at least 8 chars long. Where `n` is the positional indicator for the parameter.
  * `606` - expand.`n` in query should be one of [ChildItems Restocks StoresList SalesLocations DeliveryPrice CollectPrice DisplayLocations DeliveryTime FoodAvailabilities]. Where `n` is the positional indicator for the parameter.

details.description: Additional error details. These change depending on the context of the query.

traceId.description: Unique trace identifier for debugging and log correlation
